purpose: T+L R1 Probe 1: CPU n-gram memory with 131K buckets
notes: 131K buckets WAY too small. 100% occupancy, 93-97% collision. Need 4M+ buckets.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 1: CPU n-gram memory with 4M buckets
notes: MODERATE signal. 14.2% bigram hit, 1.5 bpt entropy reduction. Collision still 86% — needs more buckets or better hashing for production.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 3: GPU MTP D=1 from EDSR step_2000
notes: INITIALLY appeared positive (-8.93% BPT). But matched control (below) shows MTP HURT: control reached 5.1039 vs MTP 5.2328. MTP overhead: +0.13 BPT and -10% throughput.

purpose: Matched control for MTP probe: 1000 more steps from step_2000, NO MTP
notes: CRITICAL: Control BEATS MTP probe. BPT 5.1039 (control) vs 5.2328 (MTP) = MTP adds +0.13 BPT overhead. MTP hurts at 98M scale — validates Meta finding. Throughput also 10% better without MTP (38K vs 34K tok/s).

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 6: Differentiable 3-exit halting controller from EDSR step_2000
notes: FALSIFIED. Control (5.1039) beats halting (5.257) by +0.153 BPT. All exits WORSE than control (exit3: 7.14 vs 6.22, exit7: 5.66 vs 5.20). Throughput 21% slower (29.8K vs 37.7K). Mixture loss + halting overhead hurts at 98M scale. ~17% of tokens routed to earlier exits but quality degraded.

purpose: T+L R2 Probe: Engram-style n-gram memory fusion at layers 1/4/7 from EDSR step_2000
notes: FALSIFIED. Control (5.1039) beats memory (5.237) by +0.133 BPT. Gate barely opened (0.127 vs initial 0.119 = sigmoid(-2)). 5% throughput overhead. Fourth consecutive probe where plain control wins at 98M. Pattern definitive: at 98M, ALL auxiliary modules hurt — even CPU-resident memory with zero-init warm-start.

purpose: Continue plain EDSR control from step 3000 to 5000 with WSD cooldown
notes: Strong improvement from WSD cooldown. BPT 5.1039→4.7252 (-7.4%). Exit-8 at 5000 (4.8141) beats full-depth at 3000 (5.1039). LR decayed from 3e-4 to near-zero. Model is approaching 98M capacity limit at this data budget.

purpose: lm-eval benchmark sweep on control step_3000
notes: Large gaps to Pythia-160M (300B tokens) and SmolLM2-135M (2T tokens). Our model trained on ~2.8B tokens (3000 steps × 512 ctx × ~1800 tok/step). Data gap is 100x+. PIQA and WinoGrande closest to baselines. LAMBADA worst (6.6% vs 35%+ — needs more data for memorization).

purpose: Post-hoc exit threshold calibration on control step_3000

metrics.sweet_spot: p30 entropy threshold: 10% compute savings at +0.013 BPT

notes: STRONG POSITIVE for O5. Post-hoc thresholds give meaningful compute savings with minimal quality loss. 10% of tokens have near-zero entropy at exit-8 and can exit with +0.0008 BPT cost. Even 30% early exit costs only +0.013 BPT. This validates fixed exits + post-hoc calibration as the right strategy for inference efficiency.

purpose: lm-eval benchmark sweep on control step_5000 (partial: 5 of 7 tasks)
notes: CORRECTED TOKEN ACCOUNTING: 5000 steps × 16384 tok/step = 81.92M tokens (NOT 2.8B). All benchmarks improved vs step 3000: HellaSwag +0.14%, PIQA +1.04%, ARC-Easy +1.22%, ARC-Challenge +1.02%, SciQ +3.40%. Missing: lambada_openai, winogrande (timed out or errored). Gap to Pythia-160M is ~3660x in training tokens, making these results impressive per token.

purpose: Fix critical token accounting error across all documentation
notes: Codex T+L R3 identified discrepancy. Previous '~2.8B tokens at step 3000' was wrong by ~57x. Actual: 49.15M tokens. This changes benchmark gap interpretation from ~100x to ~6100x at step 3000, ~3660x at step 5000. The model is FAR more undertrained than previously stated.

purpose: Net2Wider expansion of 98M step_5000 checkpoint to 197M model
notes: Net2Wider duplication 768->1024 + 2 new layers with zero-init residual gates. 196.7M params. Ready for 200M training when GPU is free (after 98M hits step 10000). Needs parity verification via det-eval before training.

purpose: Teacher-guided shard scoring with llama3.2:3b for O4 data efficiency
notes: Teacher scoring complete. Differentiated weights: eli5/poetry/writingprompts upweighted (2.0x), fineweb_s0/openwebmath downweighted (0.5x). Mean weight ~1.0. Ready for A/B test: uniform vs weighted sampling from same 98M parent. Interesting: openwebmath scored LOW — teacher found it less informative than expected, possibly due to notation-heavy content being hard to score via text quality prompts.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 1: Compare DyT vs RMSNorm normalization on matched 42M models
notes: RMSNorm wins BPT (5.08 vs 5.83, +0.75). DyT converges slower but gap closes from 2.2 to 0.75 by step 5000. DyT kurtosis starts extreme (29.3) but drops to 1.5 — much higher than RMSNorm (1.0). Max activations converge (20.6 vs 21.6). DyT doesn't bound residual stream — only internal activations. Generation: RMSNorm more coherent. Verdict: DyT needs more training time or init tuning at 42M scale.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 2: Compare TOP auxiliary loss vs plain NTP on matched 42M models
notes: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. TOP at weight=0.05 with activation at step 200 destroys training at 42M scale. BPT 9.68 vs 5.07 (+4.6 BPT worse). Kurtosis explodes to 99.3 max (vs 1.15). Max activations 134 vs 22. TOP loss magnitude (~20) comparable to CE (~8), creating ~12% gradient contribution that conflicts with NTP. Definitive kill for TOP at this scale/weight. TOP needs much lower weight (0.001) or much later activation (step 2000+) if retried.

purpose: T+L R2 Probe 1: Optimizer/norm comparison (3 variants x 5000 steps)
notes: V2 (AdamW+SS-RMSNorm) WINS: best BPT 4.91, best kurtosis, lowest max_act. SS-RMSNorm matches RMSNorm (+0.08 BPT edge) with fewer params. Muon (lr=0.02) LOSES at 42M: faster early convergence (1.7x at step 1500) but periodic instability (regressions at steps 2000, 3500-4000), final BPT 0.10 worse than V2, max_act 2.7x higher. Muon lr=0.02 too aggressive for 42M scale.

purpose: T+L R3 Probe: Trunk choice — pure transformer vs pure conv vs inter-layer hybrid (3A+9H) at 42M
notes: MAJOR POSITIVE: Inter-layer hybrid (3A+9H) WINS by 0.19 BPT over pure transformer with fewer params AND better activation health. Conv-only loses by +0.70. Hybrid gets best of both worlds: attention for exact retrieval + conv for efficient local mixing. Strongest positive signal for hybrid architecture. Intra-layer parallel (every block has BOTH) expected to be even stronger.

purpose: T+L R4 Probe: Intra-layer parallel hybrid (mean fusion, P blocks) at 42M. k=64 vs k=4, both 9L.
notes: k4 BEATS k64 by 0.15 BPT with dramatically better activation health (kurtosis 0.82 vs 1.45, max_act 18 vs 21). k4 nearly ties inter-layer hybrid (4.80 vs 4.72) with only 9 layers vs 12, and fewer params. Strong evidence: (1) small kernels win in intra-layer blocks, (2) intra-layer is higher quality per-layer, (3) 12L concat-project should beat inter-layer. Validates R4 intuition #2.

purpose: R4 microprobe: kernel sweep (k4/k16/k64) on concat-project + ratio comparison (1:1 vs 2:3)
notes: KEY FINDINGS: (1) In concat, k16 best BPT, k4 best stability, k64 catastrophic kurtosis. (2) 2:3 ratio beats 1:1 by 0.06 BPT at +7% params. (3) ALL concat variants show instability (kurtosis 1.4-5.8). (4) Mean fusion k4 at 9L (4.80) beats best concat at 12L (4.83) with 2.4x better stability. (5) concat_2to3_k16 nearly ties inter-layer hybrid (4.77 vs 4.72).

purpose: MiniPLM difference scoring pilot: 200 windows, 10% corpus sample, Qwen3-1.7B teacher vs Qwen3-0.6B reference
notes: Infrastructure validated. Pipeline works end-to-end on CPU. Teacher consistently has lower NLL than reference (gap 0.26). Source ranking sensible: literature/wiki highest, chat lowest. Ready for full 10% scoring when GPU is free.

purpose: R5 100M promotion gate: 24x512 transformer vs 24x512 HEMD-R5-G normalized additive hybrid
notes: MIXED result. Hybrid wins BPT by 0.11 (passes >=0.10 threshold) but FAILS stability criterion: kurtosis 3.94 vs 1.66 (2.4x worse), max_act 63.6 vs 44.8 (1.4x worse). Normalized additive fusion improved over raw concat but still unstable at 100M scale. Per-branch norm + beta NOT sufficient � need stronger stabilization (e.g., post-fusion norm, gradient clipping on betas, or smaller beta init). Hybrid learns faster early (step 1000-1500) but advantage erodes.

purpose: R6 stabilization mini-gate: R6-F (no beta, SS-RMSNorm) vs R6-S (softmax mixing) vs transformer baseline at 100M scale
notes: BOTH FAIL. R6-F (no beta): BPT 4.78, kurtosis 3.5, max_act 58.5. R6-S (softmax): BPT 4.77, kurtosis 2.6, max_act 55.0. Transformer: BPT 4.67, kurtosis 1.7, max_act 41.6. ALL fail success criteria. Root cause: projected branches (256->512) accumulate magnitude through depth, fused_rms shrinks from 0.36 to 0.08 as branches diverge in direction. β was NOT the main instability source.

purpose: MiniPLM scoring: 500 windows, 10% sample (expanded from 200-window pilot)
notes: Consistent with 200-window pilot. Pipeline validated at 500-window scale. Source ranking stable. Infrastructure ready for full-corpus scoring.

purpose: R7-P sidecar probe: post-fusion SS-RMSNorm hybrids at 100M. 24G all-hybrid + 6G+18A mixed schedule.
notes: 24G: FAIL (BPT 4.91 worse than transformer, kurtosis 2.5 > 2.1 threshold, max_act 75.6). 6G+18A: PARTIAL PASS - BPT 4.686 BEATS transformer by 0.071, kurtosis 2.0 within 2.1 threshold, BUT max_act 58.5 > 52 threshold. Key insight: limiting G-blocks to early layers (1-6) + attention for rest WORKS for learning quality. Conv instability is contained to early layers but activation magnitude still bleeds through. Post-fusion norm delayed instability by ~1000 steps vs R6 variants.

purpose: R7 mainline: P-GQA full-dim hybrid vs transformer at 100M. Last hybrid test before kill rule. transformer_24x512 vs pgqa_24x512 vs pgqa_mixed_8q16a.
notes: P-GQA best BPT 4.729 vs transformer 4.757 = +0.028 improvement, BELOW +0.05 threshold. All variants stable (kurtosis <2.1, max_act <52). Architecture decision: PURE TRANSFORMER LOCKED. Hybrid gain insufficient to justify complexity.

purpose: O4 first decision probe: raw data vs MiniPLM source-level reweighted data on identical pure transformer 24x512. Tests whether data selection improves convergence.
notes: Queued after R7 probes. Success: MiniPLM variant achieves lower BPT at step 5000.

purpose: First RMFD KD probe: 2-arm test (control, single Qwen3-1.7B teacher). Tests whether representation-level KD (state CKA + semantic relational) improves over pure NTP.
notes: COMPLETED. Rep KD = HEAD-START ONLY. Final delta -0.008 BPT (noise). KD led early (delta -0.059 at step 2500) but control caught up during WSD decay phase. Arm 3 ABORTED per Codex. KD arm has elevated kurtosis (17.6 vs 4.8) and max_act (80.5 vs 69.0) — potential stability concern at longer training.

purpose: 15K benchmark gate: control vs mechanism-fixed logit KD. Codex-designed inverted-U alpha (0.10->0.60->0.10->0.0), rising tau (1.5->3.0), confidence gating. Logit-only, no rep. From 5K warm-start. THE decisive test of whether scheduled logit KD persists at 90M:1.7B ratio.
notes: Control arm running. Step 10000: BPT=4.420 (-0.022 from 9K, decelerating as expected in late flat-LR). Curve: 4.895→4.824→4.680→4.632→4.612→4.616→4.538→4.498→4.442→4.420. Kurtosis 51.9 (step 9K spike 227.1 confirmed TRANSIENT). Named checkpoint saved. 67% through control arm. WSD decay starts at step 12K.

purpose: 4-arm KD surface ablation: control vs rep-only(CKA+semantic) vs logit-only(cross-tokenizer ETA) vs rep+logit. Tests which KD surface provides persistent benefit beyond head-start. Total alpha=1.0 for all KD arms.
notes: RUNNING. Arms 1-2 COMPLETE. Arm 2 FINAL: +0.018 WORSE than control (4.516 vs 4.498). Rep KD = HEAD-START ONLY (confirmed 3rd time). Full trajectory: +0.038→-0.002→-0.094→-0.130→+0.004→+0.018. Gap peaked at -0.130 mid-training, collapsed during WSD decay. Control consolidated 1.8x better (-0.329 vs -0.182). Healthier activations (kurtosis 4.5 vs 5.0, max_act 61 vs 77) don't translate to better BPT. Arm 3 (logit_only): ACTIVELY HARMFUL. +0.257@500, +0.184@1000, +0.272@1500, +0.273@2000. Gap plateaued at ~+0.27 — constant penalty, no knowledge transfer. KD loss oscillates 1.6-2.2 without convergence. Cross-tokenizer logit KD at α=1.0/T=2.0/1:19 = constant ~0.28 BPT penalty (not head-start, persistent harm). KD loss oscillated 1.5-2.2 without convergence. Arm 3 FINAL: 4.783 BPT (+0.285 vs control 4.498). Arm 4 @500: BPT=5.169 (+0.147 vs control), kurtosis=2.5 (LOWEST of all arms), max_act=50.4. Logit penalty ~57% of arm 3's (not halved as predicted). BUT stabilization is remarkable — rep KD IS helping activations even if not BPT. Arm 4 @1000: BPT=5.039 (+0.128 vs control 4.911). Penalty narrowed only -0.019 in 500 steps (arm 2 recovered -0.040 in same interval). Kurtosis rose 2.5→3.4 (stabilization advantage LOST). Max_act 50.4→57.9. Rep stabilization was TRANSIENT — only visible in first 500 steps. Combined arm now tracks logit-only penalty pattern with ~50% amplitude. Arm 4 @1500: BPT=4.940 (+0.110 vs control 4.830). Penalty narrowing: +0.147→+0.128→+0.110. Kurtosis 3.9 (control=4.9). Arm 4 @2000: REGRESSION (4.940→4.979). IF spiked to 2.14 (plateau amplification). Arm 4 @2500: Recovery (4.979→4.893), IF=1.26. Gap widened to +0.210 despite lower IF. Kurtosis 5.8. Arm 4 FINAL @3000: BPT=4.651 (+0.153 vs control 4.498). IF=1.01 (additivity restored at low LR). Kurtosis=12.4 (ALARMING — 2.5x control). Full IF trajectory: 0.99→1.41→1.24→2.14→1.26→1.01. Conclusion: multi-surface KD at extreme ratios is toxic (plateau amplification, kurtosis instability). Logit-only with inverted-U alpha confirmed for 15K gate.
